EPISODE_NARRATIVE: North northwest swells between 8 to 10 feet affected the north facing coast of Puerto Rico resulting in dangerous Rip Currents and High Surf.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Man drowned near Bahia Beach area of Rio Grande.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: North to northwest swells of 8 to 10 feet produced dangerous surf conditions and rip currents along the north facing coast of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Man drowned at Condado beach near La Concha hotel.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture due to an upper level low combined with daytime heating and local effects to produced showers with isolated strong thunderstorms over the western and central interior and coastal sections, with a number of areas of heavy rain needing an urban and small stream advisory.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: PREMA relayed information from 911 call center about possible thunderstorm wind damages at Barrio Mora, road 112, Isabela. Wind damages were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very weak low level wind flow and a broad upper level jet resulted in strong convection which remained nearly stationary for few hours. Showers moved through the northeastern portions of PR including the San Juan metro area. Some urban and small streams advisories were issued for this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Person trapped in car on flooded road 181 intersection with road 199 in front of Walgreens and JF Montalvo.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed trees ar Road PR 167 near Jose Antonio Davila school. at Volcan street in Hato Tejas sector and at road PR 167, Km 18.4.  Power lines were downed at Reparto Teresita, Road 38.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds and seas were below small craft advisory conditions, but winds approached 20 kts around PR and mariners needed to exercise caution.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 53 year old man was carried away by rip currents at 3 Tubos Beach in Joyudas Cabo Rojo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moisture associated with departing tropical wave and the proximity of an upper level low continued to result in showers and thunderstorms across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at PR-2 near El Buen Caf�� Rest.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Available moisture combined with isolated thunderstorms over the western interior and northwest sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three residences were affected at Urbanizacion El Culebrinas by flooded waters in San Sebastian.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds up to 20kntos and seas up to 6 feet affected the regional waters, with occasional  higher seas. Small craft operators were urged to exercise caution across most of the local waters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A man was dragged by a rip current.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough in combination with limited moisture, diurnal heating and local island effects resulted in scattered to locally numerous showers across northern and western sections of Puerto Rico. Urban flooding was reported across the municipality of Mayaguez.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at Litoral Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough in combination with limited moisture, diurnal heating and local island effects resulted in scattered to locally numerous showers across northern and western sections of Puerto Rico. Urban flooding was reported across the municipality of Mayaguez.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at road PR-102 at Restaurant Carb��n de Palo.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough in combination with limited moisture, diurnal heating and local island effects resulted in scattered to locally numerous showers across northern and western sections of Puerto Rico. Urban flooding was reported across the municipality of Mayaguez.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding reported at exit to road PR-2 near Isidoro Garcia Stadium in Mayaguez.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Northerly swells reached the local Atlantic waters resulting in large breaking waves and dangerous rip currents. A high surf advisory was in effect for the north facing coasts of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 44 year old male drowned in the Pe����n Brusi Beach in Camuy as he was pulled out to sea by rip currents. A 25 year old male was also rescued by the local emergency managers rescue team with minor injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers affected portions of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgins Islands due to the remnants od a frontal boundary across the forecast area. An urban and small stream flood advisory was issued for San Juan and vicinity area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at University Gardens, at intersection with Colombia and Notre Dame streets.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Surface high pressure ridge affected the region. Showers were observed along the north coast of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Large breaking waves and dangerous rip currents affected the north facing coasts of Puerto Rico and the Northern U.S. Virgin Islands resulting in dangerous surf conditions. A high surf advisory was issued until 10 pm AST.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A surfer lost his life surfing at the Tres Palmas Beach in Rincon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Surface high pressure north of the region maintained moderate to fresh trade winds. Buoys indicated seas of 4 to 5 feet across Caribbean and Atlantic waters. Small craft operators were urged to exercise caution.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 29-year old man from Poland and professor at University of Puerto Rico was carried away by rip currents ad Guajataca Beach in the county line between Quebradillas and Isabela.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central, moving north northeast, under a south to south southwest wind flow. Deep tropical moisture affected the local region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rio Fajardo out of its banks. PR-3 with PR-53 at Cilito ward, impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central, moving north northeast, under a south to south southwest wind flow. Deep tropical moisture affected the local region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A mudslide was reported in the intersection between roads PR-167 and PR-812 in Bayamon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper level trough remained over the region developing into an upper level low north of the region. Scattered to numerous showers developed over the area, affecting mainly the eastern interior and southeast sections of Puerto Rico. These showers and thunderstorms produced torrential rainfall which led to numerous flash flooding across these areas. This event aid in the hydrological drought which started in May 2015 lasting through the end of October. Rationing of water was ended in Carraizo and La Plata Basins due to this rainy event.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several homes were flooded in some areas of Urb Vista San Lorenzo, Urb. San Lorenzo Valley, Barrio Jagual and Barrio Quemado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered to numerous showers with thunderstorms developed over the Cordillera Central of Puerto Rico. Heavy showers slowly moved east over the Sierra de Luquillo with rainfall accumulations between 1 and 2 inches in some localized areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mudslide reported at road PR 139 k.m 7.9.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough across the Eastern Caribbean dominated the region resulting in isolated showers and thunderstorms affecting the surrounding waters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large breaking waves generated strong rip currents at Condado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: TUTT continued to become more amplified south and west of the region, as a high pressure ridge aloft shifted farther east across the Lesser Antilles. Tropical wave surrounded by a large area of moisture and disorganized convection.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong rip currents were observed at Luquillo Beach.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave resulted in an increase in moisture to the local area. Showers and thunderstorms affected the local waters as well as across the local islands. An upper level trough to the west of the region, contributed to the development of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail of 0.25 inches approximately was reported in Adjuntas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating and a tropical wave passage enhanced afternoon convection across the interior and northwest quadrant of Puerto Rico. Showers and thunderstorms produced minor urban flooding in urban and poor drainage areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Outflow from a strong thunderstorm which produced heavy rainfall amounts between 1 and 1.5 inches, also generated a strong wind gust resulting in large broken and downed tree limbs and branches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating and a tropical wave passage enhanced afternoon convection across the interior and northwest quadrant of Puerto Rico. Showers and thunderstorms produced minor urban flooding in urban and poor drainage areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm moving across the San Juan Metro Area, produced a max wind gust up to 40 mph reported at San Juan International Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level ridge affected the area. A TUTT established east of Leeward Islands. Low level moisture increased as a weak tropical wave moved mainly south of the islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding pictured on Twitter over Ave. Nativo Alers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms were observed across many sectors of PR. Thunderstorms which produced periods of heavy rainfall, resulted in minor urban and rural flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong wind damage was reported near road PR-2 at Victoria Plaza. The roof was damaged by the strong winds. When the winds ripped the roof activated the building fire sprinkler system flooding local government offices. National Lumber and Hardware store sign was damaged by the wind and a few articles that were on display also were damaged by the winds. Although estimated winds were around 50 to 60 mph were reported no conclusive tornado evidence was found.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough combined with light winds at the surface and favorable upper level atmospheric conditions to produce isolated strong thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several reports of marble size hail were reported in Humacao.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Tropical Storm Erika affected Puerto Rico on August 27th 2015. Several winds damages associated with Tropical Storm force winds were reported. Several trees were downed as well as power lines. 250,000 people were without power. Agriculture sector damages were estimated around 17.37 million dollars. Dozens of electrical power poles were reported damages.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees downed at route 66, km 9.9 in Canovanas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Available moisture combined with isolated thunderstorms over the western interior and northwest sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: PR Emergency management agency reported that road 125 in San Sebasti��n was flooded. R��o Culebrinas was out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low northwest of Puerto Rico moved across the central Caribbean resulting in scattered showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at San Antonio sector.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Available moisture combined with isolated thunderstorms over the western interior and northwest sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: PR emergency management agency reported that road 119 in San Sebastian was flooded. Rio Culebrinas was out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms with heavy showers developed across the south central Atlantic and low pressure moved into the western Atlantic affecting the local area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Low lying areas in PR'2 from San German to Hormigueros were reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers with isolated thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central resulting from a surface low across the Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two lanes of Tunel Minillas were flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers with isolated thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central resulting from a surface low across the Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One lane of PR-2 near Walgreens pharmacy in Aguadilla, was reported flooded.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 125 km. 17.9 flooded and impassable due to Rio Guatemala, a tributary of Rio Culebrinas, going out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers with isolated thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central resulting from a surface low across the Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several homes and business parking lots downtown Moca, along calle Barbosa, were flooded. Also, emergency managers reported several mudslides/debris flow in urban and rural areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers with isolated thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central resulting from a surface low across the Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rio Culebrinas went out of its bans around 8:30 pm AST, affecting Barrio Cruz and Barrio Maria. Sectors Cruz isleta, Coco and Jimenez were affected by the river.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers with isolated thunderstorms developed across the Cordillera Central resulting from a surface low across the Atlantic.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roads 418 and 439 were impassable due to Rio Culebrinas, which went out of its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough persisted to the north of the local area resulting in numerous showers with scattered thunderstorms across the northern half of Puerto Rico. Very heavy rain with excessive lightning were observed over this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Guajataca lake overflows due to excessive runoff from Rio Guajataca. Road 119 impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough persisted to the north of the local area resulting in numerous showers with scattered thunderstorms across the northern half of Puerto Rico. Very heavy rain with excessive lightning were observed over this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Persisten heavy rain resulted in a mudslide in Barrio Plana, road 446.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough persisted to the north of the local area resulting in numerous showers with scattered thunderstorms across the northern half of Puerto Rico. Very heavy rain with excessive lightning were observed over this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road 115 partially flooded in downtown Rincon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough persisted to the north of the local area resulting in numerous showers with scattered thunderstorms across the northern half of Puerto Rico. Very heavy rain with excessive lightning were observed over this area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Persistent heavy rains resulted in a mudslide at road 110 near El Mirador.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moderate north northeast swell of 4 to 6 feet impacted the Atlantic waters and Caribbean Passages.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large breaking waves produced numerous rip currents at Condado.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A band of low level moisture moved over the forecast area resulting in scattered showers affecting portions of the local islands and U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-5 from Cata��o to Bayam��n was reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Vigorous showers and thunderstorms grew during the afternoon hours over western PR due to local effects.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Vega Alta PREMA officers reported closed roads 647 and 620, due to a surge of water along Rio Cibuco.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough lingered over the area. This feature produced favorable atmospheric conditions for the development of showers and thunderstorms. These showers and thunderstorms produced very heavy rainfall over Adjuntas municipality, where soil were already saturated.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mudslide completely covered road 129 at km 38.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Vigorous showers and thunderstorms grew during the afternoon hours over western PR due to local effects.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall across Mayaguez and vicinity lead to several mudslides in rural areas reported along road 354, sector Rio Canas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A tropical wave resulted in numerous showers and thunderstorms developed along Cordillera Central and across Western and interior sections of Puerto Rico. The wave was accompanied by a large area of moisture with showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-1 was reported flooded near El Pulguero in direction to San Juan.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of lingering moisture associated with a wave, low level instability and the local effect resulted in convective activity mainly across the interior and northwestern PR.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pea sized hail observed in Barranquitas at Quebrada Grande sector.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A TUTT affected the local islands with mid level high pressure building in from East. Strong thunderstorms began developing across the central interior with hail and strong gusty winds reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed trees at Guilarte sector blocking road 131.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A band of low level moisture moved over the forecast area resulting in scattered showers affecting portions of the local islands and U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Kennedy Avenue was reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A band of low level moisture moved over the forecast area resulting in scattered showers affecting portions of the local islands and U.S. Virgin Islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road in front of Federal Prison was reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level trough affected Puerto Rico producing thunderstorms with heavy rain followed by relatively moist east southeast flow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Urban flooding was reported at Litoral sector.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms developed over north central Puerto Rico. Light low level winds maintained these showers nearly stationary over Hatillo,producing very heavy rainfall in a short period of time.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Road PR-2 from Hatillo to Arecibo was reported flooded in front of Sams Club Store.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with daytime heating and orographic effects to produce scattered to numerous showers with strong thunderstorms affecting mainly north central and western sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: PR-2 in front of SAMS club in Hatillo was reported as impassable due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with daytime heating and orographic effects to produce scattered to numerous showers with strong thunderstorms affecting mainly north central and western sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed tree on road PR-2 after Mayaguez Resort.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level trough combined with daytime heating and orographic effects to produce scattered to numerous showers with strong thunderstorms affecting mainly north central and western sections of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Downed tree in Barrio Tierras Nuevas in Manati.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong trade winds produced dangerous marine conditions across the Atlantic waters north of Puerto Rico.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Couple was fishing from a boat when a wave capsized.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid to upper level trough continued meandering west of the local islands through the week. An induced trough across the northeast Caribbean promoting light to moderate southeast winds and resulting in shower activity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Emergency manager official from Naguabo reported that Rio Blanco flooded exit 22 on road 31 and had to rescue a stranded motorist.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and thunderstorms developed across the west interior and northwest Puerto Rico due to a mid-upper trough affected the local region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rio Culebrinas was reported out of its banks at road PR-404. Also, Coloso valley was flooded.
